creator: jim@jmock.com
text:
On Mon, 21 Aug 2000 at 14:22:07 +0100, sketchy@netcraft.com wrote:
> >Number:         20743
> >Category:       ports
> >Synopsis:       ports
> >Confidential:   no
> >Severity:       non-critical
> >Priority:       low
> >Responsible:    freebsd-ports
> >State:          open

[snip..]

> >Description:
> 
> Stable release (1.4.2) of the popular X11 Gtk/GNOME IRC Client.  The
> current version in ports (1.5.6) is:
> 
> o Development code
> o (Optionally) linked against the buggy gdk-pixmap library (at least the
>   xchat transparency effects used by it aren't complete, and not as stable
>   as XTEXT).
> o Based on newer perl wrappers, breaking all existing scripts.  This can
>   (and probably will) be changed even more during 1.5.x development cycle.
> o Not meant for user-consumption

There are a few things of note here.

   1)  A while back I asked if anyone was interested in having an
       xchat-devel port and an xchat port (see the archives).  The
       response I got back indicated no.

   2)  Naming it xchat14 is a bad idea.  The stable version isn't going
       to be 1.4.x forever.  What do you plan on doing when it's 1.6.x?
       You'd have to get a repo copy each time it changed.  I can't
       think of too many committers who'd be too excited about this.

I'm willing to take the xchat port back down to the stable version, but
I'm not psychic.  If people are having problems with 1.5.x, they need to
speak up so I'll know.

That said, I'm going to close this PR later today.  For now, I'm going
to take ownership of it since a) xchat is one of the ports I maintain,
and b) it can't be committed as it stands.

- jim
